root_text.sn22.152:1.2: “Kismiṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, sati, kiṁ upādāya, kiṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn22.152:1.3: ‘so attā, so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’”ti?
root_text.sn22.152:1.5: “Rūpe kho, bhikkhave, sati, rūpaṁ upādāya, rūpaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn22.152:1.6: ‘so attā, so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’ti.
root_text.sn22.152:1.10: viññāṇe sati, viññāṇaṁ upādāya, viññāṇaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn22.152:1.11: ‘so attā, so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’ti.
root_text.sn22.152:2.4: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn22.152:2.6: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn22.152:2.7: ‘so attā, so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’”ti?
root_text.sn22.152:2.14: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn22.152:2.16: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn22.152:2.17: ‘so attā so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’”ti?

translation_text.sn22.152:1.2: “Mendicants, when what exists, because of grasping what and insisting on what, does the view arise:
translation_text.sn22.152:1.3: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable’?”
translation_text.sn22.152:1.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn22.152:1.5: “When form exists, because of grasping form and insisting on form, the view arises:
translation_text.sn22.152:1.6: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable.’
translation_text.sn22.152:1.10: consciousness exists, because of grasping consciousness and insisting on consciousness, the view arises:
translation_text.sn22.152:1.11: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable.’
translation_text.sn22.152:2.4: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn22.152:2.6: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would the view arise:
translation_text.sn22.152:2.7: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable’?”
translation_text.sn22.152:2.14: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn22.152:2.16: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would the view arise:
translation_text.sn22.152:2.17: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable’?”
translation_text.sn22.152:2.20: They understand: ‘… there is nothing further for this place.’”
